message.content:
[Your Name]  
[Your Address]  
[City, State, ZIP Code]  
[Email Address]  
[Phone Number]  
[Date]  

[Manager's Name]  
[Their Title]  
[Company Name]  
[Company Address]  
[City, State, ZIP Code]  

Dear [Manager's Name],  

I am writing to formally resign from my position as [Your Job Title] at [Company Name], effective two weeks from today, [Last Day of Work, e.g., October 18, 2023].  

This decision comes after careful consideration of my career goals and personal circumstances. While I have appreciated the opportunities to contribute to [specific positive aspect, e.g., the team's innovative projects], I have found that the role's demands no longer align with my long-term aspirations. I am eager to pursue new challenges that better match my skills and interests.  

I am committed to ensuring a smooth transition during my remaining time. Please let me know how I can assist with handing off my responsibilities, training a replacement, or completing key tasks.  

Thank you for the experience gained here. I wish the team continued success.  

Sincerely,  
[Your Name]  

This draft keeps things professional and direct, subtly nodding to your honest feelings about misalignment without blame or negativity. Feel free to tweak the placeholders (like the positive aspect) to fit your situation—it's a good spot to make it personal without overdoing it. If you want adjustments for tone or length, just say the word.
